title: Zebkit Utility Class Manifest
description: Canonical, hand-authored contract for a group of utility-class families. Encodes the grammar (base x edges x values expanded against token scales) rather than enumerating classes, except for keyword families whose values are hardcoded CSS keywords by design. generate:utilities renders each family's source SCSS partial from this contract; lint:utilities keeps the round trip honest. Consumed by docs and editor support.

key.description: Manifest key, unique across utility manifests. Usually the owning token module key.

description.description: What this group of utilities is for, written for both humans and tooling.

domain.description: Agent-context domain this manifest belongs to. Domain files group related utility grammar without fragmenting it per manifest.

layer.description: CSS @layer the generated rules are wrapped in (default for all families; a family can override). Zebkit currently emits all utilities into 'utilities'.

statePatternProjection.description: A reusable projection of token values onto one or more CSS properties. The {target} placeholder names the class-facing target; additional axes let the selected token role vary independently from that target.

targets.description: Maps a class target to its CSS property or properties, e.g. {"fill": "fill", "stroke": "stroke"}.

axes.description: Value axes used only by this projection, merged with the statePattern's common axes.

class.description: Class template using {target}, common axes, and projection axes.

var.description: CSS variable template using the same bindings as class; segment order may differ.

varSource.description: When 'scss', U2 var integrity checking is skipped because the variables are emitted by a Sass mixin rather than a TypeScript token module.

rule.description: One CSS rule emitted verbatim: a free-form selector, its declarations, and optional nested rules ('&' allowed).

selector.description: Free-form CSS/SCSS selector, e.g. ".focusable:focus, .focusable:focus-visible", "[class^='transition-']", ":root", or a nested "&.transition-slow". Any '.class' token contributes to the predicted class set; element/attribute/pseudo selectors predict nothing.

declarations.description: property -> value, emitted literally. Custom properties (--zbk-*) may be set (defining local cascade variables) and referenced via var(--zbk-*); generate rewrites the --zbk- prefix to the configurable prefix var at emit time. Every var(--zbk-*) reference must resolve to a real token or a --zbk-* custom property the family itself sets (lint U2).

nested.description: Nested rules, typically using '&' for compound state overrides (e.g. '&.transition-slow').

name.description: Family key, e.g. 'margin', 'overflow-x'. Unique across all utility manifests.

description.description: What the family does, in one or two sentences.

guidance.description: Usage rules a design tool or AI must respect ('prefer gap on the parent over per-child margins').

source.description: Repo-root-relative SCSS file(s) generated for / linted against this family. The lint diffs the family against the union of these files.

properties.description: CSS properties the classes set, e.g. ['margin-block', 'margin-inline'].

layer.description: Overrides the manifest-level layer for this family.

important.description: True when the rules use !important. Zebkit relies on @layer ordering instead; avoid unless a family genuinely needs it.

a11y.description: How this family responds to zebkit's accessibility dials, e.g. 'values rescale with the spacing a11y dial'.

tier.description: Default usage tier for AI tooling and docs when choosing from this family. Omit = recommended. 'situational' = valid but needs a specific reason; 'discouraged' = avoid unless there is no alternative (e.g. 'order', whose visual reordering diverges from DOM/focus order). Tiers are advisory metadata — they never change generated CSS, only how tooling ranks choices.

valueTiers.description: Per-value overrides of the family tier, keyed by pattern value or class name (e.g. {'vertical-text': 'discouraged'}). Only list values that differ from the family default. The lint checks each key is a real value/class of the family.

classes.description: Explicit class list for keyword families whose values are hardcoded CSS keywords (display-flex, position-sticky). Mutually exclusive with 'pattern' and 'rules'.

rules.description: Verbatim CSS/SCSS rule blocks for families whose grammar is not class x value: CSS-variable-setting classes with compound/nested selectors, pseudo-class rules (.focusable:focus), and element-selector prose in @layer base. Mutually exclusive with 'classes' and 'pattern'. Rendered verbatim; the predicted class set is every '.class' token appearing in the selectors (element/attribute/pseudo selectors predict nothing). 'modifiers' is not supported on rules families.

unlayered.description: Rules families only: emit these rules outside any @layer, for foundational :root cascade-variable defaults that sit outside the layer stack (e.g. the transition timing/duration defaults). Ignored by 'classes'/'pattern' families.

statePattern.description: Grammar for token-value projections that emit base classes and interaction-state prefixed forms. Common axes define the token vocabulary; each projection independently maps class targets to CSS properties and declares class/token templates. modifiers is forbidden — state coverage is controlled by states.

axes.description: Named value axes shared by every projection. null in a value array marks an optional axis (maps to the {-axis} placeholder in templates); the axis is omitted when null.

projections.description: One or more mappings from class targets to CSS properties and token values. Projection axes augment the common axes, allowing the CSS property and selected token role to vary independently.

states.description: true = all five canonical interaction states (base, focus, hover, active, disabled). An array restricts to the named subset.

pattern.description: Grammar for scale-driven families. Expands to '<base>[-<edge>][-neg]-<value>' and is diffed against the source SCSS by the lint.

edges.description: Optional edge/axis segment values; zebkit prefers logical edges, e.g. ['block','inline','block-start','inline-end']. The edgeless form is always included unless edgeRequired is true.

edgeRequired.description: True when the bare '<base>-<value>' form does not exist.

values.description: Value suffixes. With 'tokens', each value V must be a key in the bound token module and resolves to var(--zbk-<tokens.varPrefix>-<V>). OPTIONAL when 'tokens' is bound (without edgeInToken): omit it to auto-derive the full value list from the token group (filtered to tokens.types when set, neg-* keys excluded) so it can never drift from the tokens. When present, it is the source of truth — a deliberate subset/limiter the lint still validates against the group. Required for keyword (no-tokens) and edgeInToken families.

negativeValues.description: Values also emitted as '<base>[-<edge>]-neg-<value>', consuming the module's 'neg-<value>' tokens (zebkit defines negative spacing as first-class tokens). Set to true to auto-mirror: emit a negative for every resolved positive value that has a matching, type-allowed neg-<value> token (values without one, like named sizes or '0', are skipped). Useful where negatives make sense (margin) and simply omitted where they don't (padding). Requires 'tokens' bound without edgeInToken.

exclude.description: Token values to drop from this family's value set (both positive and negative), applied after values are resolved/derived. Lets a family take the whole group minus a few keys instead of enumerating every wanted value. Each entry must be a real token key in the bound group (the lint flags typos).

literals.description: Extra non-token values added to the positive axis, mapped to verbatim CSS — e.g. {'auto': 'auto'} yields .margin-auto, .margin-inline-auto, ... using the family's edges, edgeProperties, and responsive modifiers. Lets one token-driven family also emit keyword classes (auto, inherit) without a second family. They skip token resolution and negative mirroring; bake in '!important' if needed. A literal key may not collide with a token name in the bound group (the lint flags it).

aliases.description: Full alias class -> full canonical class, e.g. {'gap-sm': 'gap-05'}. Aliases share the canonical rule.

tokens.description: Token binding for value axes. Omit for keyword families.

group.description: Token module key the values draw from (the module's exported `key`), e.g. 'spacing'. The lint checks each pattern value is a real key in that module.

varPrefix.description: CSS custom property prefix after --zbk-, e.g. 'spacing' for --zbk-spacing-md.

types.description: Restrict valid pattern values to token keys whose declared `type` is in this list. Use when one token group mixes purposes — e.g. the spacing group holds both `type: spacing` (margin/padding) and `type: sizing` (width/flex-basis) tokens; a flex-basis family sets types: ['sizing'] to admit card/aside/tablet and reject section-margin-block. Values mapped in generator.valueMap are literal CSS and exempt.

edgeInToken.description: True when the edge segment is part of the token name: '<varPrefix>-<edge>-<value>' rather than '<varPrefix>-<value>'.

modifiers.description: Prefix axes that multiply the family's classes. Declare only what should exist.

hover.description: True when every class also exists as 'hover:<class>:hover'.

responsive.description: Breakpoint prefixes under which every class is re-emitted, e.g. ['tablet','desktop'] for 'tablet:margin-md'. Must be keys of the $breakpoints map in core/styles/variables/_breakpoints.scss.

knownExceptions.description: Documented irregularities: classes the grammar predicts but the SCSS lacks, or vice versa. Full class names including any modifier prefix. Shrink these; never grow them to make a build pass.

extra.description: Classes present in the SCSS beyond the declared grammar.

missing.description: Classes the grammar predicts that the SCSS does not emit.

generator.description: Optional rendering directives used by generate:utilities to emit utility SCSS from this family contract.

edgeProperties.description: Maps edge key to declaration properties. Use '' for the edgeless form.

valueMap.description: Verbatim final CSS values. Keyed by value suffix, neg-<value>, or full class name (for aliases that consume their own alias var).

declarations.description: Explicit class -> declaration map for keyword (classes) families.

rawScss.description: Verbatim SCSS block emitted for classes that need bespoke rule shapes.

staticScss.description: Verbatim SCSS emitted once with the base rules and never replicated under breakpoint prefixes.
